text: contrasted Ronin. Devon was one of them; he fed and clothed the women who caught his interest, for a time, but only in return for sex. And only while they held his fickle attention. When he was bored or annoyed, he cast them aside, leaving them with nothing but the shirts on their backs . How many times had Lara irritated Ronin? How many times had she given him reason to be angry at her? If he were like Devon, he’d have beaten her and cast her out long before . The house had felt so empty while Ronin was away. She missed having his eyes on her, missed his calm, sometimes frustrating conversations, missed him . Crazy as it seemed on the surface, she had been worried about him. The Dust was dangerous for anyone. By his own admission, he wasn’t impervious to harm. How might Lara have reacted if he’d taken a bullet that knocked out one of his primary systems? If he’d left her and never come back again, and she never knew his fate ? What if Warlord did to Ronin what he’d done to Tabitha and her bot? Ronin hadn’t told her the details, and Lara wasn’t sure she wanted them, but knowing their fate was enough. Cheyenne was not safe . Her heart constricted, and she pressed a hand over her chest. The dread such thoughts instilled in her meant something, didn’t it ? “What’s wrong , Lara ?” She started, turning her head to see Ronin standing in the bedroom, naked, his skin still damp. Licking her lips, she struggled for words. “I think we should leave .” “We have enough food to last us days, at least,” he replied, brow furrowed . “Leave Cheyenne, Ronin .” “The Dust is too dangerous, Lara. I know you want to come on runs with me , but — ” “I mean for good. We should leave Cheyenne forever .” He stood perfectly still and silent. Thinking. It looked unnatural, but she wasn’t unsettled by it anymore . “Where would you like to go?” he finally asked . “I don’t know. You’ve been all kinds of places, so I thought…” She dropped her hands into her lap and sighed. “It’s dangerous here. I know it was Warlord and his gearheads who killed my sister. And you can’t tell me I’d be safe if I set one foot outside this house without you .” “I could tell you that,” he said, looking at the floor, “but it wouldn’t be true .” “It’s not your fault, but I feel like a prisoner here, Ronin. I don’t want to just sit here and wait for something to happen to me, like it did to her.” Lara bit her lip, dropping her gaze to the ring as she twisted it on her finger. “If you don’t want to go, at least take me past the wall, and…and I’ll find my way to somewhere else .” Ronin crossed the distance between them and settled a hand on her shoulder, taking her chin in the other. He gently guided her eyes up to his. “I made a vow , Lara .” “I don’t want you held by words, Ronin .” “I’m not held by them. I’m… enriched by them.” His palm moved up to cup her cheek. “There’s nothing tying either of us to this place, apart from each other. My only bond is with you .” Lara’s stomach fluttered. “And what does that bond mean to you ?” “It means I am alive .” “But you can have that without me. You already were, before I was even born.” Fortunately, her disappointment didn’t transfer much into her voice. He felt alive with her. That wasn’t really proof of any emotional attachment. It wasn’t a declaration of something… more . He felt obligated to protect her, enjoyed rolling around in bed with her, and thought — for now — that talking to her was more interesting than talking to himself . Why should she hope for anything greater than that? Wasn’t it enough ? “I don’t think that I was,” he said, brushing his thumb over her cheekbone . Lara settled her hand atop his and closed her eyes. “Let’s leave tonight. We can get the hell out of here, before anyone notices .” “ No .” Something sank in her belly, dragging her remaining hopes down with it. She should’ve known that hope only led to heartache by now . “Why not?” She dropped her hand. His fingers twitched lightly against her cheek . “We need supplies . “We have supplies, and you have all the stuff you just brought in to trade for more. And we can sell this, too.” She tugged at the ring . Ronin covered her hands in both of his before she could get the ring off. His gaze was steady when she looked up into his eyes. Her heart was pounding, her breath heavy, and she didn’t know why. There wasn’t anything left for her in this town but hopelessness, fear, and death. She had to leave . “No, Lara. Keep it.” He pushed the ring back onto her finger. “The nearest settlement outside Cheyenne is about eighty miles, depending on the route. I can make that in a day. With you, it’ll be four or five days, depending on the weather .” “You don’t think I should go because I’d fucking slow you down?” Lara pulled her hand, but Ronin didn’t release it . “That’s not what I’m saying , Lara .” “Sure as hell sounds like you are !” “That’s four days, minimum, of food and water. If a bad storm system blows through, it could delay us another week on top of that .” “I’ve gone a long time without food before. I can do it again .” “I don’t doubt it, but I’m not going to take that risk. We need to bring at least ten or twelve days-worth of food
